operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot selection highlights the chosen robot and opens robot details
operational_knowledge_units.summary: When the Robot option is selected in the UI, a user can select a robot to view its details. The selected robot is visually indicated by a box around it.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "When Robot option is selected, select a robot to view robot details."
Slide text: "The robot selected will have a box around it showing its selected."

relationships.evidence: Selecting a robot is described as the action used to view robot details.
The selected robot will have a box around it.

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot Selection Overview: when Robot option is selected, choose a robot to view robot details. The selected robot is shown with a box around it.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Tote status is displayed beneath robot ID with special markers for removed robots or missing totes
operational_knowledge_units.summary: If a robot has a tote, the tote is shown under the robot ID. A tote prefixed with a question mark indicates the robot has been removed but the tote remains. If a robot does not have a tote, the UI displays "layer_1."
observed_evidence: Slide text: "If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot Id."
Slide text: "If the tote has a “?” before it, that means the robot has been removed but the tote has not."
Slide text: "If a robot doesn’t have a tote it will say layer_1"

relationships.evidence: Tote appears under the robot ID when present.

relationships.to: Robot removed but tote not removed
relationships.evidence: The slide explicitly defines this meaning.
The slide states that robots without totes show layer_1.

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot tote display behavior: tote appears under robot ID. A '?' before the tote means the robot has been removed but the tote has not. If no tote is present, the display says layer_1.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot color coding communicates connection and task status
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The UI uses robot colors to indicate robot condition or activity: purple for disconnected, blue or flashing blue for in task/searching for label, green for not in a task, and red for fault or low battery.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "Blue/Flashing Blue: robot in task/searching for label"
Slide text: "Green: robot not in a task"
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot color meanings in Robot Selection Overview: purple = disconnected, blue or flashing blue = in task/searching for label, green = not in task, red = fault or low battery.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Select a robot to view robot details
preconditions: The Robot option is available in the UI.
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes Robot Selection overview and indicates that selecting a robot shows details and places a box around the selected robot.

steps.instruction: With the Robot option selected, select a robot in the UI.
steps.expected_outcome: The selected robot's details are shown.
steps.validation_check: A box appears around the selected robot.
steps.visual_region_hint: Robot selection area showing robot entries and selection highlight

procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Navigation attempt: when Robot option is selected, select a robot to view robot details. Validation is a box around the selected robot.
